     The Numbers Business Report

Whiplash (2014)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $13,892,940Details
International Box Office $25,911,303
Worldwide Box Office $39,804,243
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $1,496,231
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $5,912,937
Total $7,409,168
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Andrew Neyman is an ambitious young jazz drummer, single-minded in his pursuit to rise to the top of his elite east coast music conservatory. Plagued by the failed writing career of his father, Andrew hungers day and night to become one of the greats. Terence Fletcher, an instructor equally known for his teaching talents as for his terrifying methods, leads the top jazz ensemble in the school. Fletcher discovers Andrew and transfers the aspiring drummer into his band, forever changing the young man’s life. Andrew’s passion to achieve perfection quickly spirals into obsession, as his ruthless teacher continues to push him to the brink of both his ability—and his sanity.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$135,388 (1.0% of total gross)
Legs:17.65 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:34.9% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$3,300,000 (worldwide box office is 12.1 times production budget)
Theater counts:6 opening theaters/695 max. theaters, 9.1 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $19,678,087

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: October 10th, 2014 (Limited) by Sony Pictures Classics
September 20th, 2024 (Limited) by Sony Pictures Classics
International Releases: October 17th, 2014 (Wide) (Australia)
October 23rd, 2014 (Wide) (New Zealand)
October 23rd, 2014 (Wide) (Australia)
December 26th, 2014 (Wide) (France)
January 2nd, 2015 (Wide) (Poland)
Video Release: February 24th, 2015 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for strong language including some sexual references.
(Rating bulletin 2319, 4/23/2014)
Running Time: 106 minutes
Award nomination: 2015 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Film festival: Sundance Film Festival 2014
Geographical setting: New York, New York City
Plot point: Bad Teachers, Emotional Abuse, Musicians, Obsession, Relationships Gone Wrong, Romance
Social setting: College, Jazz
Subgenre: Life Drama
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Blumhouse, Right of Way Films
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Andrew Neyman is an ambitious young jazz drummer, single-minded in his pursuit to rise to the top of his elite east coast music conservatory. Plagued by the failed writing career of his father, Andrew hungers day and night to become one of the greats. Terence Fletcher, an instructor equally known for his teaching talents as for his terrifying methods, leads the top jazz ensemble in the school. Fletcher discovers Andrew and transfers the aspiring drummer into his band, forever changing the young man’s life. Andrew’s passion to achieve perfection quickly spirals into obsession, as his ruthless teacher continues to push him to the brink of both his ability—and his sanity.
